title: Grid tracks and placement

publication.changeSummary: Technology-specific CSS: Responsive and Accessible Styling draft for Grid tracks and placement; independent standards, browser, pedagogy, and accessibility review required. Beginner-quality contract 1.0.0 applied deterministically from existing source-linked draft facts; publication stage and reviewer state are unchanged.

sources.locator: CSS Snapshot 2024; W3C Working Group Note 2024; provisions applicable to Grid tracks and placement
sources.claim: Grid defines explicit or implicit rows and columns, places items into grid areas, and sizes tracks with fixed, intrinsic, flexible, and minmax functions.

sources.locator: MDN CSS Guide; accessed 2026-07; provisions applicable to Grid tracks and placement
sources.claim: Grid defines explicit or implicit rows and columns, places items into grid areas, and sizes tracks with fixed, intrinsic, flexible, and minmax functions.

canonicalExplanation.summary: Grid defines explicit or implicit rows and columns, places items into grid areas, and sizes tracks with fixed, intrinsic, flexible, and minmax functions.

sections.heading: Start here
sections.body: Start with this case: A card grid uses repeat(auto-fit,minmax(min(100%,18rem),1fr)) to create bounded responsive columns. Your goal is to implement and inspect a two-dimensional page or component grid. Work through the example one step at a time, then use the boundary to check your answer.

sections.heading: Before you begin
sections.body: Review these declared prerequisites first: Normal flow and display; Intrinsic sizing and constraints. If one is unfamiliar, revisit it before continuing.

sections.heading: Technology rule
sections.body: Grid defines explicit or implicit rows and columns, places items into grid areas, and sizes tracks with fixed, intrinsic, flexible, and minmax functions. Apply this rule to the actual markup, stylesheet, JavaScript, or component state rather than memorizing its name.

sections.heading: Boundary and browser reality
sections.body: Implicit tracks can appear unexpectedly, fr shares leftover space after minimums, and dense placement can change visual order. This draft therefore rejects: 1fr means one equal fraction of the entire container before content minimums are considered.

includes: Define explicit and implicit tracks with repeat, minmax, lines and named areas.
Implement and inspect a two-dimensional page or component grid.
excludes: Implicit tracks can appear unexpectedly, fr shares leftover space after minimums, and dense placement can change visual order.

outcomes: Implement and inspect a two-dimensional page or component grid.

examples.title: Grid tracks and placement: concrete case
examples.situation: A card grid uses repeat(auto-fit,minmax(min(100%,18rem),1fr)) to create bounded responsive columns.
walkthrough: Step 1 — Inspect the exact artifact and starting state: A card grid uses repeat(auto-fit,minmax(min(100%,18rem),1fr)) to create bounded responsive columns.
Step 2 — Use the core rule above to predict one observable result.
Step 3 — Verify the correction: Flexible fr tracks share leftover space after track minimums and other sizing contributions are resolved.
examples.result: The result follows because Flexible fr tracks share leftover space after track minimums and other sizing contributions are resolved.

Step 3 — Reject the misconception: 1fr means one equal fraction of the entire container before content minimums are considered.
examples.result: The boundary requires this correction: Flexible fr tracks share leftover space after track minimums and other sizing contributions are resolved.

misconceptions.diagnosticPrompt: Use a concrete CSS: Responsive and Accessible Styling artifact to explain why this belief fails: 1fr means one equal fraction of the entire container before content minimums are considered.

analogy.example: A layered stage design can model Grid tracks and placement, where rules size, place, and paint existing content.
usefulFor: Building a first mental model for Grid tracks and placement before using exact platform terms
limitations: Physical layers do not reproduce cascade algorithms, intrinsic sizing, writing modes, or user preferences.
